sections.paragraph:
ϑόρυβος, ου, ὁ [cp. ϑρέομαι ‘cry aloud, shriek’] ‘noisy disruptive activity’, uproar, clamor, tumult

—a. as generated by an excited group or crowd threatening political disturbance Ac 20:1; 21:34; 24:18; at the level of a riot Mt 26:5; 27:24; Mk 14:2.

—b. as generated by a crowd or group engaged in traditional gestures of lamentation Mk 5:38.
